strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded and high-throughput backend, including microservices handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic — directly relevant to the distributed-systems and real-time inference demands of the role.

Current Staff Engineer work at re:cinq on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, showing hands-on engagement with exactly the agentic tooling stack the CTO role centers on.

Fintech and regulated-environment exposure at RIDE Capital (German investment platform) plus pricing systems at Optilyz, combined with founder experience scaling GISCollective — evidence of both domain familiarity and end-to-end ownership.
concerns: Title trajectory peaks at Staff/Senior IC rather than VP/CTO, with no explicit record of scaling an org from 10 to 30+ engineers.
Multi-agent AI production experience is recent (since April 2025) rather than spanning multiple years of operating such systems at scale.
No explicit evidence of board-level communication or governance of outsourced/hybrid delivery partners.

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic AI and MCP infrastructure work
Distributed systems and real-time backend depth

response.justification: Aisha combines deep distributed-systems craftsmanship — including microservices processing millions of requests per second — with current, hands-on work building agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, which is precisely the technical core of this CTO mandate. Prior tenure at RIDE Capital and Optilyz demonstrates comfort in regulated fintech and pricing-critical environments, while founding and operating GISCollective shows the end-to-end ownership, architectural breadth, and disciplined engineering culture (TDD, trunk-based development, observability) that this role demands.
